mnemonics: JVM's Dynamic Decision: Java Virtual Machine decides which method to run at runtime, not compile time.
Reference Type vs. Object Type: The reference is the 'name tag' (compile-time), but the object is the 'actual person' (runtime).
keyPoints: Dynamic Method Dispatch is the mechanism by which a call to an overridden method is resolved at runtime, based on the type of the object being referred to.
It is a core principle of runtime polymorphism in Java.
It only works with method overriding, using a superclass reference variable to hold a subclass object.
commonMistakes: Trying to use Dynamic Method Dispatch with data members (variables) instead of methods. Variable access is resolved at compile-time based on the reference type.
Forgetting that the method must be overridden in the subclass. If the subclass does not override the method, the superclass version is called.
